message:
fix(virtual_tcp): enforce backpressure and partial-write semantics

poll_write unconditionally extended the VecDeque without checking
against VIRTUAL_TCP_CHANNEL_CAPACITY_BYTES, allowing unbounded memory
growth and hiding flow-control bugs in application code under
deterministic testing.

Fix:
- Check buffer capacity before writing; return Pending if full
- Accept partial writes up to remaining capacity (matches real TCP)
- Add write_waker field to ChannelHalf for reader→writer notification
- Reader wakes write_waker after draining to unblock the writer
- Drop wakes both wakers via take_wakers()

Also fixes task_inspector.rs test compilation (create_task return type
change from 4-tuple to 2-tuple, cx.checkpoint() removed).

22 virtual TCP tests pass including backpressure scenarios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
